danger_signals.visual_form: Devil creatures with distinct sprite design appearing in Tower of Ancients
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural threat; possession danger; early indication of demon king Zeon's influence

danger_signals.visual_form: Previously sealed door standing open, visual break in dungeon progression
danger_signals.meaning: Ancient seal compromised; magical containment failure; major world-state threat

danger_signals.visual_form: King character model showing signs of possession (likely visual distortion or altered appearance)
danger_signals.meaning: Political leadership compromised; kingdom vulnerable to invasion; authority corrupted

danger_signals.visual_form: Galam forces occupying Granseal territory; foreign soldier sprites in domestic spaces
danger_signals.meaning: Inter-kingdom conflict active; political sovereignty threatened; safe zones breached

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated buildings where player can inspect allies and manage forces
safety_signals.meaning: Sanctuary zones; strategic planning spaces; respite from combat threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Dedicated healer class with healing spell abilities
safety_signals.meaning: Recovery capability available; survivability support; party sustainability

safety_signals.visual_form: Visible and hidden treasures scattered in explorable environments
safety_signals.meaning: Resource accumulation; progression safety net; reward for exploration reducing scarcity

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Granseal soldiers (friendly initially, later under Galam control)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Granseal Castle; shrine south of castle with magical seals; Tower of Ancients as governmental/historical landmark

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Galam military forces; distinct soldier sprites compared to Granseal troops
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Galam territory; dungeon where party is imprisoned; King Galam's court

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Gizmos as signature enemy type; Zeon as demon king leader
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Gizmos (standard demons); Possessed rulers (corrupted version of human enemies); Oddeye (Zeon's chief follower)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tower of Ancients (primary stronghold); sealed shrine (containment location); areas affected by possession and curse

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Force Sword (artifact goal); Jewels of Light and Darkness (faction objective markers)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle headquarters; mobile across Parmecia continent; recruitment locations for ally acquisition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tower of Ancients sealed and closed; King Granseal healthy; kingdoms at stable relations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs conduct normal routines; no military presence in towns; exploration is safe

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tower door unlocked and standing open; Gizmo enemies appear; magical disturbances visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gizmos attack on sight; King Granseal falls ill; emergency response from court advisers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: King character model altered/corrupted; demonic influence visible on possessed ruler; hostages taken
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Possessed kings command armies against their own kingdoms; allies held captive; battle objectives include exorcism

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Galam military forces occupy Granseal spaces; foreign soldier sprites in domestic locations; Tower becomes contested zone
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party captured and imprisoned; forced dungeon escape; battles against foreign military; kingdoms divided

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Granseal citizens migrate to Parmecia continent; new settlement zones; environmental shift from original homeland
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Free roaming expanded across new continent; increased exploration scope; ally recruitment from new regions

environmental_storytelling.example: Tower of Ancients transitions from sealed/closed to unlocked/open—visual marker of the central plot catalyst without exposition
King Granseal moves from throne to sick bed to possessed state—character sprite location and condition convey plot progression and threat escalation
Galam military presence in Granseal locations shows political collapse and invasion through visual occupation rather than dialogue
Dungeons, prisons, and collapsed areas create visual barriers that reflect political imprisonment and structural damage from conflict
Gizmo concentration around Tower of Ancients and possessed rulers establishes connection between specific locations and demonic threat
Each ally unit has visual class identity (healer Sarah, knight Chester, squire Bowie) allowing immediate role recognition in party management

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Spring forth to attack on sight; leader Gizmo flees after defeat, signaling intelligence and hierarchy
Immediately possesses next target (King Granseal → King Galam); demonstrates adaptability and continued threat despite tactical defeat
Commands military; makes political decisions; maintains kingdom order
Becomes enemy combatant; holds hostages; corrupted judgment reflected in military aggression
Invade Granseal; capture player party; become possessed kingdom's military arm
Investigates supernatural incidents; performs exorcism; demonstrates authority hierarchy and magical countermeasures
Unwittingly breaks seal through theft; later aids party escape from dungeon; demonstrates character redemption through mechanical alliance shift

notes: Shining Force II communicates world-state primarily through environmental changes, NPC availability states, and enemy type presence. The core narrative shift—seal broken → possession → invasion → continental relocation—is visually manifested through dungeon accessibility, character sprite conditions, and military occupation. Faction identity relies on spatial territories and enemy unit types rather than explicit color/sigil systems. The game uses character class visual distinction for party management communication, and grid-based tactical positioning to show political/military conflicts. The possession mechanic is a key danger signal, transforming familiar NPCs into threats and corrupting political authority visually.
